4295	\begin{lemma}[Degree-0/1 sheaf-axiom equalizer of a sheaf on a finite standard cover]
4296	  \label{lem:qcoh_section_equalizer}
4297	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.qcoh_section_equalizer}
4298	  Let \(X = \Spec R\), let \(\mathcal{F}\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module (a sheaf of modules on the
4299	  spectral site), let \(W \subseteq X\) be an open, and let \(g_1, \ldots, g_n \in R\) be a finite family
4300	  with \(W = \bigcup_{j=1}^{n} \bigl(W \cap D(g_j)\bigr)\). Then the augmented two-term {\v C}ech sequence
4301	  \[
4302	    0 \longrightarrow \Gamma(W, \mathcal{F})
4303	      \xrightarrow{\ \rho\ } \prod_{j} \Gamma\bigl(W \cap D(g_j), \mathcal{F}\bigr)
4304	      \xrightarrow{\ \delta\ } \prod_{j,k} \Gamma\bigl(W \cap D(g_j g_k), \mathcal{F}\bigr)
4305	  \]
4306	  is exact at both nonzero terms: \(\rho\) — the product of the restriction maps — is injective, and a
4307	  family \((s_j)_j\) lies in the image of \(\rho\) iff \(\delta\bigl((s_j)_j\bigr) = 0\), where
4308	  \(\delta\) is the difference of the two overlap-restriction maps and
4309	  \(W \cap D(g_j g_k) = W \cap D(g_j) \cap D(g_k)\). Equivalently
4310	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact} \rho\, \delta\) holds together with the injectivity of \(\rho\).
4311	\end{lemma}
4312	\begin{proof}
4313	  This is nothing but the sheaf axiom for \(\mathcal{F}\), specialised to the finite open cover
4314	  \(\{W \cap D(g_j)\}_{j}\) of \(W\) and read off in degrees \(0\) and \(1\). The sheaf of modules
4315	  \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(\Spec R\) is a sheaf, so its underlying presheaf satisfies the gluing axiom for
4316	  every open cover. For the cover \(\{W \cap D(g_j)\}_j\) of \(W\) the gluing axiom says exactly two
4317	  things. First, a section of \(\mathcal{F}\) over \(W\) is determined by its restrictions to the
4318	  \(W \cap D(g_j)\): this is the injectivity of \(\rho\). Second, a \emph{matching family} — a tuple
4319	  \((s_j)_j\) whose members agree on the pairwise intersections
4320	  \(W \cap D(g_j) \cap D(g_k)\), which for basic opens are again basic opens \(W \cap D(g_j g_k)\) —
4321	  glues to a (necessarily unique) section over \(W\): this is the surjectivity of \(\rho\) onto
4322	  \(\ker \delta\). Reading the two restriction-to-overlap maps as the two cofaces of the {\v C}ech nerve
4323	  and \(\delta\) as their difference turns ``matching family'' into the equation
4324	  \(\delta\bigl((s_j)_j\bigr) = 0\), giving the asserted exactness. Only the sheaf condition on the cover
4325	  \(\{W \cap D(g_j)\}_j\) is used; no positive-degree {\v C}ech vanishing enters.
4326	\end{proof}
4327	
4328	\begin{lemma}[Localisation preserves exactness]
4329	  \label{lem:localized_module_map_exact_mathlib}
4330	  \lean{IsLocalizedModule.map_exact}
4331	  \mathlibok
4332	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
4333	  Let \(S \subseteq R\) be a submonoid of a commutative ring and let
4334	  \(M_0 \xrightarrow{\,a\,} M_1 \xrightarrow{\,b\,} M_2\) be \(R\)-linear maps with
4335	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact} a\, b\). Fix, for each \(i\), a localisation map \(\ell_i : M_i \to M_i'\)
4336	  at \(S\) (so each \(M_i'\) is the localised module \(S^{-1} M_i\) and
4337	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule} S\, \ell_i\)). Then the induced maps
4338	  \(S^{-1} a : M_0' \to M_1'\) and \(S^{-1} b : M_1' \to M_2'\) again form an exact sequence:
4339	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact} (S^{-1} a)\, (S^{-1} b)\). In other words localisation at \(S\) is an
4340	  exact functor; in particular it carries short exact sequences to short exact sequences and commutes
4341	  with the formation of kernels.
4342	\end{lemma}
4343	
4344	\begin{lemma}[Per-tile section localisation at \(f\)]
4345	  \label{lem:tile_section_localization}

4347	  \uses{lem:qcoh_finite_presentation_cover, lem:presentation_modulesRestrictBasicOpen,
4348	    lem:section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation, lem:restrict_obj_mathlib}

4350	  and let \(g \in R\) be either a cover element \(g = g_j\) or an overlap product \(g = g_j g_k\) of the
4351	  finite presentation cover of Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_finite_presentation_cover}, so that the tile
4352	  \(\mathcal{F}_{(g)} = \operatorname{modulesRestrictBasicOpen} g\, \mathcal{F}\) on \(\Spec R_g\) admits
4353	  a global presentation. Then the restriction-of-sections map
4354	  \(\Gamma(D(g), \mathcal{F}) \to \Gamma(D(gf), \mathcal{F})\) exhibits its target as the localisation of
4355	  its source at the powers of \(f\): \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{powers} f)\) holds
4356	  for it. Equivalently there is a canonical \(R\)-linear isomorphism
4357	  \(\Gamma(D(g), \mathcal{F})_f \cong \Gamma(D(gf), \mathcal{F})\).
4358	\end{lemma}
4359	\begin{proof}
4360	  \uses{lem:presentation_modulesRestrictBasicOpen, lem:section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation,
4361	    lem:restrict_obj_mathlib}
4362	  Since \(D(g)\) lies in a presentation member \(U_{\varphi}\) of the cover, step B4
4363	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:presentation_modulesRestrictBasicOpen}) presents the tile \(\mathcal{F}_{(g)}\)
4364	  globally over \(\Spec R_g\). Apply Lemma~\ref{lem:section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation} to the
4365	  globally presented \(\mathcal{F}_{(g)}\) and the image \(\bar f\) of \(f\) in \(R_g\): the
4366	  section-restriction map
4367	  \(\Gamma(\Spec R_g, \mathcal{F}_{(g)}) \to \Gamma(D(\bar f), \mathcal{F}_{(g)})\) is a localisation at
4368	  the powers of \(\bar f\). Under the definitional section comparison of
4369	  Lemma~\ref{lem:restrict_obj_mathlib} — \(\Gamma(\mathcal{F}_{(g)}, V) = \Gamma(\mathcal{F}, \iota(V))\)
4370	  for the open immersion \(\iota : \Spec R_g \cong D(g) \hookrightarrow X\) — the source is identified
4371	  with \(\Gamma(D(g), \mathcal{F})\) and the target with
4372	  \(\Gamma(D(g) \cap D(f), \mathcal{F}) = \Gamma(D(gf), \mathcal{F})\), and the powers of \(\bar f\)
4373	  correspond to the powers of \(f\). Hence the section-restriction map
4374	  \(\Gamma(D(g), \mathcal{F}) \to \Gamma(D(gf), \mathcal{F})\) is an
4375	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) for the powers of \(f\).
4376

4378	  \(\mathcal{F}\) is associated to a module (the tile is globally presented, hence tilde), and never on

4391	  Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_finite_presentation_cover}, with \(\operatorname{span}\{g_j\} = R\). Then the
4392	  canonical \(R\)-linear map \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \to \Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\) induced by
4393	  \(\rho_f : \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \to \Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\) (the localisation lift of \(\rho_f\)
4394	  at the powers of \(f\)) is an isomorphism.
4395	\end{lemma}
4396	\begin{proof}
4397	  \uses{lem:qcoh_section_equalizer, lem:localized_module_map_exact_mathlib,
4398	    lem:tile_section_localization}
4399	  Write the degree-\(0/1\) equalizer (Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_section_equalizer}) for \(\mathcal{F}\) on the

4401	  \[

4403	      \xrightarrow{\delta} \prod_{j,k} \Gamma(D(g_j g_k), \mathcal{F}),
4404	  \]
4405	  and the same equalizer for the induced cover \(D(f) = \bigcup_j \bigl(D(f) \cap D(g_j)\bigr)\) of
4406	  \(D(f)\) (take \(W = D(f)\), using \(D(f) \cap D(g_j) = D(g_j f)\)):
4407	  \[
4408	    0 \to \Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F}) \xrightarrow{\rho'} \prod_j \Gamma(D(g_j f), \mathcal{F})
4409	      \xrightarrow{\delta'} \prod_{j,k} \Gamma(D(g_j g_k f), \mathcal{F}).
4410	  \]

4415	      \xrightarrow{\delta_f} \prod_{j,k} \Gamma(D(g_j g_k), \mathcal{F})_f
4416	  \]

4420	
4421	  For every cover element \(g_j\) and overlap product \(g_j g_k\), the per-tile localisation
4422	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:tile_section_localization}) supplies canonical \(R\)-linear isomorphisms
4423	  \[
4424	    \Gamma(D(g_j), \mathcal{F})_f \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \Gamma(D(g_j f), \mathcal{F}),

4301	  \[

4304	      \xrightarrow{\ \delta\ } \prod_{j,k} \Gamma\bigl(W \cap D(g_j g_k), \mathcal{F}\bigr)

4307	  family \((s_j)_j\) lies in the image of \(\rho\) iff \(\delta\bigl((s_j)_j\bigr) = 0\), where
